title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Mastery Check
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an explainable constrained optimization model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.
Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (6 × 9) − (1 × 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (6 × 9) − (1 × 2).

The accepted result is within 0 of the exact value.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Optimization Ethics applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits.
